title: G3: GPoA normal n-hat and its uncertainty couple in-plane rotation into v_perp

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap.** $v_\perp = \mathbf v\cdot\hat n$ depends on the plane normal $\hat n$. Because the in-plane rotation amplitude $|\mathbf v_\parallel|$ is LARGE, an angular error $\delta$ in $\hat n$ leaks a piece $\sim |\mathbf v_\parallel|\,\delta$ of the in-plane velocity into the perpendicular channel.

**What to show.** (i) Determine $\hat n$ by a total-least-squares / PCA plane fit: form the $3\times3$ position scatter (inertia) tensor of the on-plane satellites; $\hat n$ is the eigenvector of its smallest eigenvalue. (ii) Quantify $\sigma(\hat n)$ (e.g. bootstrap over membership and positional errors). (iii) Derive the induced error term $\sigma_{v_\perp}^{(\hat n)} \approx |\mathbf v_\parallel|\,\sigma(\text{angle of }\hat n)$ and show it can reach tens of km/s for few-degree $\sigma(\hat n)$.

**Requirement.** This term must appear explicitly in the error budget (G4); omitting it overstates the significance of any $v_\perp$ detection or non-detection.
